description: Australian footballer
description_wiki: Charles Skinner (2 January 1912 – 26 November 1972) was an Australian rules footballer who played with North Melbourne in the Victorian Football League (VFL).

surface_forms: Osage County was the tall grass prairie
description: county in Oklahoma, United States
description_wiki: Osage County ( OH-sayj) is the largest county by area in the U.S. state of Oklahoma.

description: Oklahoma merchant (1864–1913)
description_wiki: Frederick Drummond (May 2, 1864 – August 22, 1913) was a Scottish-born American businessman and politician and the founding patriarch of the Oklahoma Drummond family.

description: city in Oklahoma, United States
description_wiki: Pawhuska ( paw-HUSK-ə; Osage: 𐓄𐓘𐓢𐓶𐓮𐓤𐓘, romanized: hpahúska, lit. 'White Hair'; Iowa-Oto: Paháhga) is a city in and the county seat of Osage County, Oklahoma, United States.

description: human settlement in Osage County, Oklahoma, United States of America
description_wiki: Hominy (Osage: 𐒹𐓘́͘𐓨𐓘͘𐓵𐓣͘, romanized: Hą́mąðį – night-walker) is a city in Osage County, Oklahoma, United States.

description: 1861–1865 civil war in the United States between the North and the South over the enslavement of African-Americans
description_wiki: The American Civil War (April 12, 1861 – May 26, 1865; also known by other names) was a civil war in the United States between the Union ("the North") and the Confederacy ("the South"), which was formed in 1861 by states that had seceded from the Union to preserve slavery in the United States, which they saw as threatened because of the election of Abraham Lincoln and the growing abolitionist movement in the North.

description: drove trail
description_wiki: The Chisholm Trail ( CHIZ-əm) was a stock trail and wagon route used in the post-Civil War era to drive cattle overland from ranches in southern Texas, across the Red River into Indian Territory, and northward to rail stops in Kansas.

description: federally recognized Indian tribe of the United States
description_wiki: The Cherokee Nation (Cherokee: ᏣᎳᎩᎯ ᎠᏰᎵ, romanized: Tsalagihi Ayeli or ᏣᎳᎩᏰᎵ, Tsalagiyehli) is the largest of three federally recognized Cherokee tribes in the United States.

description: federally recognized tribe in Oklahoma, US
British biochemist (1891–1952)
description_wiki: Sir Jack Cecil Drummond FRIC, FRS (12 January 1891 – 4/5 August 1952), known as a child as Jack Cecil Spinks, was a biochemist, noted for his work on nutrition as applied to the British diet under rationing during the Second World War.

surface_forms: Harvard to go to business school
aliases: Harvard University Graduate School of Business Administration
description: business school in Boston, Massachusetts, USA
description_wiki: Harvard Business School (HBS) is the graduate business school of Harvard University, a private Ivy League research university.

description: public university in Oklahoma, United States of America
description_wiki: Oklahoma State University (informally Oklahoma State or OSU) is a public land-grant research university in Stillwater, Oklahoma, United States.

description: private research university in Stanford, California, USA
description_wiki: Leland Stanford Junior University, commonly referred to as Stanford University, is a private research university in Stanford, California, United States.

description: city in Missouri, United States, that is one of two county seats of Jackson County and the largest city in Missouri
1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

description: city in Osage and Tulsa counties, Oklahoma, United States
description_wiki: Sand Springs is a city in Osage, Creek and Tulsa counties in the U.S. state of Oklahoma.

description: city in Oklahoma, United States and county seat of Tulsa County
description_wiki: Tulsa (  TUL-sə) is the second-most-populous city in the U.S. state of Oklahoma and the 48th-most populous city in the United States.

description: human settlement in Creek County, Oklahoma, United States of America
description_wiki: Bristow is a city in Creek County, Oklahoma, United States.
